Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Generator] @@clientName on resource models does not rename Resource/Collection classes\",\"body\":\"## Bug Description\\n\\n`@@clientName` applied to ARM resource models renames the **Data** and **Patch** classes correctly, but does **not** rename the **Resource** and **Collection** classes.\\n\\n## Emitter Version\\n`@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt@1.0.0-alpha.20260408.3`\\n\\n## Reproduction\\n\\nGiven a TypeSpec with `@@clientName` on a resource model:\\n\\n```typespec\\n// Resource definition\\nmodel Cluster is Azure.ResourceManager.TrackedResource {\\n  ...ResourceNameParameter;\\n}\\n\\n// In client.tsp\\n@@clientName(Microsoft.ServiceFabric.Cluster, \\u0026#34;ServiceFabricCluster\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;csharp\\u0026#34;);\\n```\\n\\n### Expected\\n\\n| Type | Expected Name |\\n|------|--------------|\\n| Data class | `ServiceFabricClusterData` |\\n| Patch class | `ServiceFabricClusterPatch` |\\n| Resource class | `ServiceFabricClusterResource` |\\n| Collection class | `ServiceFabricClusterCollection` |\\n\\n### Actual\\n\\n| Type | Actual Name | Status |\\n|------|-------------|--------|\\n| Data class | `ServiceFabricClusterData` | ✅ Renamed |\\n| Patch class | `ServiceFabricClusterPatch` | ✅ Renamed |\\n| Resource class | `ClusterResource` | ❌ **Not renamed** |\\n| Collection class | `ClusterCollection` | ❌ **Not renamed** |\\n\\n## Additional Bug: Duplicate methods generated\\n\\nIn the same ServiceFabric package, `MockableServiceFabricArmClient` and `ServiceFabricExtensions` generate **duplicate** `GetClusterVersionResource(ResourceIdentifier)` methods (identical signatures on lines 86 and 95), causing CS0111 compilation errors.\\n\\nThis appears related to the `ClusterVersionsOperationGroup` having both `get` and `getByEnvironment` operations with `@@clientLocation` set to `\\u0026#34;ClusterVersions\\u0026#34;`.\\n\\n## Context\\n\\nFound during MPG migration of `Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric` from AutoRest/Swagger to TypeSpec.\\n\\nSpec:
